title: Unicef and Seriti Institute urge businesses to join the stem job shadowing programme
description: BUSINESS NEWS - Unicef and Seriti Institute...
bodyText:
BUSINESS NEWS - Unicef and Seriti Institute are calling on businesses in the Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics (STEM) industries, to support a groundbreaking initiative aimed at addressing South Africa’s critical STEM skills shortage.


> The Unicef-STEM Job Shadowing Programme provides an opportunity for organisations to actively contribute to the development of the country’s future workforce. With Africa’s youth projected to make up 42% of the world’s youth by 2030, the need to invest in STEM education has never been more pressing.


Currently, South Africa faces a shortfall of STEM professionals, with an estimated 2.5 million engineers needed to drive the country’s infrastructure, technological advancements, and economic growth.

The programme aims to bridge this gap by offering hands-on experience to young learners aged 15 to 17 from underserved communities. Over three consecutive school holidays in 2025 and 2026, organisations will host these learners for five days per holiday, amounting to a total of 15 days.

During this time, learners will gain hands on exposure to real STEM environments, equipping them with the essential skills needed for their future careers.

Juanita Pardesi, CEO of Seriti Institute, emphasised the urgency of this initiative saying "For many young people from underserved communities, STEM careers remain abstract concepts. Without role models or exposure, becoming an engineer or data scientist may seem unattainable. This programme changes that by placing students in real-world STEM environments. We must ensure that young South Africans are prepared to enter these critical sectors. By investing in young talent today, we are building a skilled, innovative, and globally competitive workforce for the future."


> Pardesi also highlighted the vital role of businesses in shaping South Africa’s future, stating "This is not just about giving back; it’s about securing the future of our industries, our economy, and our country. Investing in STEM education today means investing in the workforce that will drive innovation and progress tomorrow."


Participating organisations will also benefit by aligning with Corporate Social Investment (CSI) and Broad-Based Black Economic Empowerment (BBBEE) objectives, while strengthening their reputation as leaders in education, youth empowerment, and workforce development.

With the programme already in motion, Seriti Institute and Unicef urge businesses, policymakers, and educators to step forward and support this transformative initiative.

"I urge organisations to take part and be part of the solution. Now is the time to act, to empower, and to build a stronger, more competitive South Africa," Pardesi added.


> So, the question remains: Will your organisation step up, or will we look back in 10 years and wonder why South Africa fell behind? The time to act is now. Let’s not waste this opportunity.


For further information on how to participate in the Unicef-STEM Job Shadowing Programme, please contact:

For more information on Job Shadowing Programme please contact:

- Raheemah Boomberg
- Programme Manager, Project & New Business Developments
- Email: [email protected]
- Mobile: 078 591 0208

Juanita Pardesi, CEO of Seriti Institute.

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
